summary: Iteration 13 — Verified backend pagination for /api/leads and /api/followups, PUT /api/users/me (route ordering + validations), and Cloudinary avatar upload/delete + auth/me reflection. Also verified frontend ProfilePage, LeadsPage pagination footer, FollowupsPage tabbed pagination, AdmissionsPage compatibility, and DashboardLayout. 18/18 backend tests PASS. Frontend smoke covered all data-testids defined for these features.

ui_bugs.issue: After successfully saving the profile via PUT /api/users/me (backend persists correctly — confirmed in pytest), the displayed profile-name element and topbar user-name still show the OLD name even after a full page reload, until a hard refresh or re-login. Likely the useAuth context (or localStorage cached user object) is not invalidated/refetched after PUT /api/users/me succeeds. Recommended fix: on successful response from PUT /api/users/me and POST/DELETE /api/uploads/avatar, call refreshAuth() / refetch /api/auth/me and update the auth context so DashboardLayout avatar + name update without manual reload.

issues: Setup-guide modal auto-opens and partially overlays the page on first visit per session — not a regression, but it intercepts clicks if a Playwright/QA test does not dismiss it first. Consider auto-dismissing once user has 1 lead + completed identity.

action_items: Refresh useAuth user state after successful PUT /api/users/me and POST/DELETE /api/uploads/avatar so the topbar avatar and profile-name update without a manual reload.
Optionally: dismiss the onboarding setup-guide modal automatically when prerequisites are met, to avoid covering profile/admin pages.
critical_code_review_comments: /app/backend/server.py is 5962 lines — violates the file-size guideline (>700). Recommend splitting into modules (auth, leads, followups, uploads, users, billing, settings, etc.). This is a long-standing tech-debt note, not new.
PUT /api/users/me route is correctly registered BEFORE PUT /api/users/{user_id} (verified by counselor flow returning 200 instead of 403). Good.
Avatar upload validates size and mime correctly (returns 400 for >800 KB and PDF). Good.

GET /api/leads and GET /api/followups now always return the paginated dict shape — confirmed. AdmissionsPage was updated to read .items with fallback, so the breaking-change blast-radius is contained.
GET /api/auth/me reflects avatar_url after upload and clears it after DELETE — confirmed via integration test.
Counselor RBAC: GET /api/leads scopes to assigned_to == current_user.id — confirmed for all returned items.

success_rate.frontend: ~90% (all required data-testids render; profile save persists in backend but UI display does not refresh in same session — minor)

test_credentials: Super Admin: admin@educationcrm.com / Admin@123. Org Admin: orgadmin@demo.com / Demo@123. Counselor: counselor@demo.com / Demo@123 (read from /app/memory/test_credentials.md).
seed_data_creation: Created 5 TEST_LeadPag leads and 3 TEST_pagination followups (today/upcoming/missed) via session-scoped pytest fixtures. All teardown deletes by _id at end of session. Also temporarily uploaded an avatar to Cloudinary for the org-admin user, then DELETEd to clear avatar_url at end of test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend P1 enhancements (pagination + avatar) are working correctly per /app/backend/tests/test_iteration13_pagination_avatar.py (18/18). The only open UI nit is that ProfilePage save does not refresh useAuth user context in the same session — the topbar avatar/name remain stale until full re-login. If the next iteration touches user-state hooks, add a refetch on PUT /api/users/me and POST/DELETE /api/uploads/avatar success.
rca of the issue: Stale auth-context after PUT /api/users/me: likely the AuthContext stores user object from /api/auth/me at login time only (and caches in localStorage). Successful PUT /api/users/me updates the DB but no re-fetch or context.setUser is triggered on the frontend, so DashboardLayout avatar (AvatarImage src=user.avatar_url) and ProfilePage profile-name (user.name) read the cached object. Mitigation: in handleProfileSave / handleAvatarUpload success branches, call refreshUser() or fetch('/api/auth/me') and dispatch into context.
